message:
refactor(fsqlite-{core,func,mvcc}): small readability touch-ups surfaced by clippy

Three tiny, unrelated clippy-surfaced fixups.

fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs
- `Expr::FunctionCall { name, args, distinct: _, .. }` is redundant —
  the `..` already ignores every unmatched field. Drop the explicit
  `distinct: _` binding to match clippy's `needless_match` guidance.

fsqlite-func/src/datetime.rs
- `parse_arithmetic_modifier`: the `else if let Some(r) = m.strip_prefix('-')`
  / `else { return None; }` ladder collapses to a `let r = m.strip_prefix('-')?;`
  inside the final `else` branch, which is both shorter and generates
  slightly better codegen (one `?` vs a manual match-return sequence).

fsqlite-mvcc/src/two_phase_commit.rs
- `COMMIT_MARKER_MAGIC` is literally the ASCII bytes of `"2PCM"`.
  Express it as `*b"2PCM"` so a human reader doesn't have to eyeball
  four byte literals to confirm the magic value.

No behavioural changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
